headline: Arm in Pole Position for AI: Liontrust's Pleydell-Bouverie
summary: Arm's move to sell its own chips has been largely cheered by investors. One of those investors supportive of the plan is Clare Pleydell-Bouverie, Fund Manager at Liontrust. Liontrust's top tech investments include Nvidia, Broadcom and TSMC. She spoke to Bloomberg's Tom Mackenzie on Bloomberg Tech: Europe.

content.title: Arm in Pole Position for AI: Liontrust's Pleydell-Bouverie
content.description:
Arm's move to sell its own chips has been largely cheered by investors. One of those investors supportive of the plan is Clare Pleydell-Bouverie, Fund Manager at Liontrust. Liontrust's top tech investments include Nvidia, Broadcom and TSMC.
She spoke to Bloomberg's Tom Mackenzie on Bloomberg Tech: Europe.
content.summary: Arm's move to sell its own chips has been largely cheered by investors. One of those investors supportive of the plan is Clare Pleydell-Bouverie, Fund Manager at Liontrust. Liontrust's top tech investments include Nvidia, Broadcom and TSMC. She spoke to Bloomberg's Tom Mackenzie on Bloomberg Tech: Europe.

headline: This Week In AI Chips - Powering AI The New ETF Revolutionizing Infrastructure Investment
summary: Wedbush Fund Advisers has launched the Dan IVES Wedbush AI Power & Infrastructure ETF (IVEP) to target companies crucial to the expanding AI infrastructure landscape. As AI technology adoption accelerates, the demand for energy and infrastructure has emerged as a significant factor, with AI workloads imposing higher energy requirements than traditional computing. IVEP aims to provide investors with access to the companies facilitating this expansion across key segments like power generation...

headline: JPMorgan Cuts Nutanix to Neutral With a $44 Target: The Cloud Infrastructure Rally May Be Cooling Off
summary: Nutanix (NASDAQ:NTNX) stock got the cold shoulder from JPMorgan on Friday, as the firm cut its rating from Overweight to Neutral and set a price target of $44. The downgrade lands as Nutanix shares have already lost significant ground, suggesting Wall Street is reassessing whether the cloud infrastructure rally has run its course for software-layer ... headline: Broadcom Higher Amid Alphabet Partnership After Earnings Beat
summary: Broadcom  flashed a death cross recently but its outlook is brightening after the stock soared past its 50-day moving average in recent days amid news of a partnership with Google parent Alphabet.  On Tuesday, the artificial intelligence chip leader revealed a new partnership with Alphabet to make tensor processing units.  Now, Broadcom stock is making its way toward a buy point of 414.61 in a consolidation base, according to Investor's Business Daily's MarketSurge.

content.title: Broadcom Higher Amid Alphabet Partnership After Earnings Beat
content.summary: Broadcom  flashed a death cross recently but its outlook is brightening after the stock soared past its 50-day moving average in recent days amid news of a partnership with Google parent Alphabet.  On Tuesday, the artificial intelligence chip leader revealed a new partnership with Alphabet to make tensor processing units.  Now, Broadcom stock is making its way toward a buy point of 414.61 in a consolidation base, according to Investor's Business Daily's MarketSurge.
